Description: Shorthand alias
Single-command entry point to run comprehensive auto-pop verification
Quick entry points for console (callable directly) Example: Debug_ValidateAutoPopulation()
Full audit: checks every blueprint string in AGS_ENTITY_TABLE
Single-civ detail: shows every key and its blueprint + exists status

Leaver Fallback Audit: validates _LEAVER_CIV_UNIQUE_FALLBACK entries resolve for every receiver civ. Reports which civ×fallback pairs would fail at runtime. Usage: Debug_BlueprintAudit_LeaverFallback()
PBG suffix dump: enumerates all Entity/Squad blueprints whose path contains suffix Usage: Debug_DumpBlueprintsForSuffix("_lan") or Debug_DumpBlueprintsForSuffix("_mon_ha_gol")
Stage 2b-1: Full BP enumeration (Entity, Squad, Upgrade path names)
Stage 2b-3: Building data (garrison capacity + base cost + build time) Iterates AGS_ENTITY_TABLE for building_ entries and extracts properties.
Stage 2b-2: Per-race squad inventory Uses World_GetPossibleSquadsCount/Blueprint to enumerate what each race can train.
Summary: quick count of extractable data (no full dump)
Stage 2b-4: Unit costs (base cost + pop cost by cap type) Iterates AGS_ENTITY_TABLE for unit_ entries and extracts cost/pop data from BPs.

Read-only validation that the auto-pop house guard is functioning correctly. Checks: auto-pop state, transfer stats counter, audit log entries, live entity scan. Returns the number of issues found (0 = PASS). Console: Debug_AutoPopHouseGuard()
Ram/Siege Tower Limit detailed state dump
Main validator: resolves all CBA_SIEGE_TABLE blueprints across all civs and runs tag validation (Phase 2) + path simulation (Phase 3) in a single pass.

Debug_FullSystemValidation(player_idx) Single console command for complete runtime validation across all subsystems. Runs: snapshot → reverse map → disconnect → deferred wait → post-validate → blueprint audit → transfer stats/timing → resource/pop/limit deltas → UI check → siege cross-val → summary
Usage: Debug_FullValidation_All() Debug_FullValidation_All(2) Debug_FullValidation_All(2, "verbose")
COMMAND 1: Full Validation (Stages 1-6) Usage: Debug_LeaverConversion_Full()  or  Debug_LeaverConversion_Full(2)
COMMAND 2: Inspect (read-only, no game state changes) Usage: Debug_LeaverConversion_Inspect()

COMMAND 5: Military Suffix Resolution Test (read-only, no spawning) Prints the suffix swap result for common unit patterns across all civs, annotated with gate outcomes (AGE-OK/BLOCK, CAP-OK/BLOCK) per candidate. Usage: Debug_LeaverConversion_MilResolve(src_idx, dst_idx)
COMMAND 3: Stress Test (multi-disconnect) Usage: Debug_LeaverConversion_Stress(2)  — disconnects 2 players sequentially

COMMAND: Surrogate Visual Test — spawns entities using arbitrary civ names, converting via _Convert_Building with civ override. Works in ANY match with ≥2 alive players; the first two alive players are auto-selected as surrogates (their actual civs are irrelevant). Usage (console): Debug_LeaverConversion_VisualByCiv("malian", "japanese", "b")
Visual test cleanup: safely drains all tracked entities/squads using the same deferred batched path as auto-cleanup. Usage (console): Debug_LeaverConversion_VisualCleanup()
Single-civ variant: audit one sender civ against all receivers.

COMMAND: Coverage Pair — detailed building-key resolution for one src→rcv civ pair. Works in ANY match (reads AGS_ENTITY_TABLE + _BLUEPRINT_REVERSE_MAP only). Usage (console): Debug_LeaverCoverage_Pair("malian", "japanese")
terminal outcome. This is the complete all-player global cross-civ route check in one command. Usage: Debug_LeaverCrossCivMatrix() Debug_LeaverCrossCivMatrix("verbose")

COMMAND: Military Coverage All Pairs — exhaustive civ×civ audit. Reports per-pair summary and global totals. opt_mode="detail" prints per-unit lines. Usage (console): Debug_LeaverMilCoverage_AllPairs() Usage (detail):  Debug_LeaverMilCoverage_AllPairs("detail")

COMMAND: Military Coverage Pair — per-unit suffix-swap + policy audit for one src→rcv pair. Works in ANY match (reads AGS_CIV_PREFIXES + _LEAVER_CIV_UNIQUE_FALLBACK + BP engine). Usage (console): Debug_LeaverMilCoverage_Pair("malian", "japanese")
Single-command deterministic MIL proof runner Usage: Debug_LeaverMilProof_OneShot()

spawning military units without age constraints and observing route selection. Usage: Debug_LeaverMilTest()                — auto-select civs, default verbosity Debug_LeaverMilTest(src_idx, dst_idx) — manual player indices Debug_LeaverMilTest(src_idx, dst_idx, verbose) — with verbose logging
PERFORMANCE DEBUG: Per-transfer timing report Prints the phase_timings and annihilation cascade count from the last completed transfer. Usage: Debug_LeaverPerformance()
PERFORMANCE DEBUG: Leaver Pipeline Status Prints current pipeline state and rule pause status. Usage: Debug_LeaverPipelineStatus()
Usage: Debug_LeaverSpawnerExemptProof()                  -- auto-select alive pair Debug_LeaverSpawnerExemptProof(src_idx, dst_idx)  -- specify by PLAYERS index

Captures both idle and mid-animation states, with transition/fallback logging. Args: threshold (default 50): synthetic inject start threshold if idle count (default 3): synthetic milestones to inject if idle timeout (default 8.0): capture timeout in seconds

Force-inject a synthetic milestone into the playback queue and start it. Use to test completion animation without waiting for kills. threshold: the kill number to show (default 50) count: how many milestones to inject (default 1)

Inject real milestones by mutating the local player's debug score by +delta, then running the canonical reward crossing path. Example: Debug_PlayerUI_RewardPlayback_InjectReal(75) adds 75 kills, enqueues any crossed thresholds, and updates the progress bar.
Force-reset the playback runner to idle (emergency stop).
Skip the current completion phase, jumping to the next phase immediately.
Print the current state of the reward playback runner.

Run a full inject-and-capture cycle with queued-icon invariant validation. Wraps rewardCaptureFull and reports PASS/FAIL for the fixed-position invariant: during center/highlight/fadeout, queued icons must stay at offsetX=0, opacity=1.0, scale=1.0.
- Print the dependency graph: each module and what it depends on.
- Return the full module list as an array of manifests (for Debug_ExportManifest serializer). @return table  Array of manifest tables
- Return the count of registered modules. @return number
- Return the tag → module mapping (for debugging dispatch routing). @return table
- Check if the registry is sealed (all imports complete). @return boolean
- List all registered modules, optionally filtered by name/tag/description substring. @param filter  string|nil  Case-insensitive substring match
- Print detailed manifest for a single module. @param name  string  Module name

- Register a debug module and its handlers. Call at the bottom of each debug domain file. @param meta      table  Module manifest (see contract above) @param handlers  table|nil  Map of function_name → function to populate debug.<namespace>
- Seal the registry and run deferred validation. Called once after all debug imports are complete (from cba_debug.scar entry point).
- Validate all module manifests: check for missing dependencies, duplicate tags, and orphan tags. Returns (issues_count, issues_list) for programmatic use. @return number, table

- Run all registered rules (or those matching a domain filter), passing optional context. @param domain_filter  string|nil  Case-insensitive substring match on domain @param ctx            table|nil   Context table passed to each rule's check function @return number passed, number total, number hard_fails
- Return the full rule list as an array (for Debug_ExportManifest serializer). @return table
- Return the count of registered rules. @return number
- List all registered rules, optionally filtered by domain. @param domain_filter  string|nil  Case-insensitive substring match
- Register a named validation rule. @param rule  table  Rule definition (see contract above)
- Print detailed info for a single rule. @param name  string  Rule name
- Print a structured report of the last Debug_CheckRules run.
- Return the full schema table (for manifest export / tooling). @return table  Array of schema entries
- Read a debug setting by key. @param key  string  Setting key (case-sensitive, e.g. "LEAVER_VERBOSE") @return any  Current value, or nil if key unknown
- Get the schema entry for a setting (for programmatic introspection). @param key  string @return table|nil  Schema entry {key, default, type, domain, description}
- List all settings, optionally filtered by domain substring. @param domain_filter  string|nil  Case-insensitive substring match on domain field
- Reset all settings to their schema defaults.
- Update a debug setting with type validation. @param key    string  Setting key @param value  any     New value (must match declared type) @return boolean  true if accepted, false if rejected

Params: question, choices, on_answer, timeout, default_choice, detail

Description: Debug_VerifyDynastiesData — Single-command verification for Dynasties data additions Validates: upgrade table entries, correction table entries, key parity, arena reward tables Console: Debug_VerifyDynastiesData()
